name: Green Pepper-And-Tomato Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 large Onion, cut into 6ths and sliced thinly crosswise
2 pounds Ripe tomatoes, peeled, seeded and chopped, juice reserved
2 medium Green bell peppers, chopped into small squares
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound the garlic in a mortar with 1 tablespoon salt until it has broken down to a paste.
2. Gradually work in the chopped basil, parsley, thyme, paprika, and tomato paste.
3. Warm the olive oil in a soup pot, add the garlic-herb paste, and mix together.
4. Add the bay leaves and onion to the pot. Sprinkle the saffron directly over the onion and stir everything together.
5. Cook for 10 minutes or until the onion has softened.
6. Add the chopped green peppers, tomatoes with their juice, and water to the pot. Bring to a boil.
7. Immediately lower the heat and simmer for 25 minutes.
8. While the soup is cooking, bring 1 1/2 cups of water to a boil. Add a dash of salt and the rice, then cook until tender (about 12-to-15 minutes).
9. Drain the rice and stir it into the finished soup.
10. Season with freshly ground pepper.
11. Serve garnished with fresh herbs and grated Parmesan cheese.
